title: Docs site as a fourth Launcher component — supervised api-reference Docusaurus on :3000
scope:
Add the api-reference Docusaurus site as a fourth supervised component in the MMFF Vector Launcher, alongside the existing tunnel / backend / frontend trio. The launcher's per-component start/stop/restart UI, JSONL logging, port-conflict detection, and adopt-existing-process behaviour all extend to the docs site so it follows the same lifecycle as the rest of the dev stack.

  • New Swift component DocsManager mirroring FrontendManager (NodeJS process supervision via npm start in api-reference/, stdout→JSONL pipe, restart policy, exit detection).
  • Extend ServiceKind enum with .docs; thread through AppState, Orchestrator, DashboardView, PortCheckView, HealthProbe.
  • Auto-start with the rest of the stack (matches existing model). Adopt an already-running docusaurus start on :3000 if present.
  • Default mode is the dev server (npm start) for hot-reload while authoring; a future phase can add a static-build mode toggle when content stabilises.
  • Health probe: HTTP GET http://localhost:3000/ with 200 OK → healthy.
  • Logs at /tmp/mmff-docs.log following the existing component-log convention.
  • Update .claude/commands/c_launcher.md from "three components" to four; add docs log path and port to the table.
value:
Today the api-reference Docusaurus site is a manual npm start in a terminal that dies the moment the CLI closes. That makes it second-class compared to the tunnel/backend/frontend, which the Launcher already supervises with restart policies and structured logs.

  • For the developer: docs are part of the dev stack — one click in the Launcher brings them up alongside everything else, and they survive terminal close, reboot, and crashes the same way the backend does.
  • For the codebase: establishes the pattern for adding a fourth supervised component cleanly, so future supervised processes (background workers, doc-generators, etc.) follow the same shape rather than each inventing its own lifecycle.
  • For the docs themselves: always-on localhost:3000 means cross-references in chat ("see /components/badge") are click-through-able without first asking the developer to remember to npm start.
implementation_plan: Story 00428 — Extend ServiceKind enum with .docs, add per-kind metadata (port 3000, label "Docs", working dir api-reference/), thread through Models.swift / AppState.swift / ServiceSnapshot. No behaviour yet — wiring only, so subsequent stories can land independently.

Story 00429 — Implement DocsManager.swift modelled on FrontendManager: spawn `npm start` in api-reference/, pipe stdout/stderr to /tmp/mmff-docs.log via JSONLLogger, exit-code handling, restart policy from RetryPolicy, port-3000 conflict detection. Adopt an already-running docusaurus start if PID is on :3000.

Story 00430 — Wire DocsManager into Orchestrator: start-all includes docs in parallel with tunnel (no dependency), stop-all includes docs, restart-all sequences correctly. Update DashboardView to render a fourth row with start/stop/restart buttons + state badge.

Story 00431 — Health probe + port-check entry: HealthProbe.swift gains a docs probe (HTTP GET localhost:3000/ → 200 healthy, !=200 or refused → unhealthy); PortCheckView lists port 3000 alongside the existing ports. Status reflected in the dashboard component row.

Story 00432 — Documentation + verification: update .claude/commands/c_launcher.md from "three components" to "four", add /tmp/mmff-docs.log to the log table, add :3000 to the PortCheckView entry. Manual smoke test: cold launcher start → all four components green; close terminal → docs still served at localhost:3000.
areas_impacted: DEV: MMFF Vector Launcher/ Swift sources (Models, AppState, Orchestrator, DashboardView, PortCheckView, HealthProbe, new DocsManager.swift)
DEV: project.yml (no manual edit needed — xcodegen picks up new Swift files automatically)
feature_list: Fourth supervised component in the Launcher: Docs (api-reference Docusaurus)
Auto-start with start-all, auto-stop with stop-all, per-component restart
Health probe via HTTP 200 on localhost:3000/
c_launcher.md updated to reflect four components
features_extended: Launcher Orchestrator: the start-all / stop-all / restart-all sequences now include docs as a fourth parallel branch (no dependency on tunnel/backend/frontend).
DashboardView: renders a fourth component row with the same control surface as the existing three.

work_item_backlog.title: DocsManager.swift — process supervision + adopt + log piping

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ServiceKind enum includes a .docs case in Models.swift
AppState exposes an observable slot for the docs component identical in shape to frontend's slot
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: DashboardView can bind to docs state without compile errors

acceptance_criteria.criterion: DocsManager.swift exists and spawns `npm start` in api-reference/
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: starting the launcher with no docusaurus running results in a `node` child PID with cwd ending in /api-reference

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Stdout + stderr from the docs process are written as JSONL to /tmp/mmff-docs.log
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: tail -f /tmp/mmff-docs.log shows valid JSON lines while the docs site is starting

acceptance_criteria.criterion: If a docusaurus start is already running on :3000 when the launcher boots, the launcher adopts it
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: DashboardView shows state `up (adopted)` with the existing PID

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Orchestrator start-all brings the docs component up in parallel with the tunnel
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: in launcher.jsonl, the docs `starting` event timestamp is within 100 ms of the tunnel `starting` event

acceptance_criteria.criterion: DashboardView renders a fourth row labelled "Docs" with the same start/stop/restart control surface
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: screenshot of the launcher dashboard shows four component rows

acceptance_criteria.criterion: HealthProbe issues GET http://localhost:3000/ and treats 200 as healthy
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: stopping the docs process flips the docs row to red within one probe interval

acceptance_criteria.criterion: PortCheckView lists :3000 alongside the existing tunnel/backend/frontend ports
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: the PortCheckView lists all four ports including :3000

acceptance_criteria.criterion: c_launcher.md updated: "three components" → "four"; /tmp/mmff-docs.log added to the log table
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: git diff shows both edits and a manual read confirms accuracy

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Closing the spawning terminal does not kill the docs process — localhost:3000 still serves the site
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: open Launcher → quit terminal → curl -sI localhost:3000 returns 200 OK

risks: Port 3000 is a popular default — collision with another local Docusaurus / React app is plausible. Mitigation: existing PortCheckView pattern surfaces the conflict; user can stop the rival process or we add a docs-port override later.
Docusaurus dev server is memory-hungry over long uptimes (react-refresh leaks). Mitigation: out of scope for v1; future phase adds a static-build mode (`npm run build && npm run serve`) toggle.
xcodegen must be re-run to pick up the new Swift file before the launcher rebuilds. Mitigation: noted in c_launcher.md; build script already calls xcodegen as the first step.
out_of_scope: Static build mode (`npm run build && npm run serve`) — deferred until docs content stabilises.
File-watcher that auto-rebuilds on .mdx change — only relevant once we move to static mode.
Custom port for the docs site — :3000 is the Docusaurus default; a port override can be added later if a real conflict surfaces.
Production deployment of the docs site — c_launcher.md is dev-stack only; production hosting is unaffected by this plan.
